work_type_list.description: An aria is a self-contained piece for one voice usually with orchestral accompaniment. They are most common inside operas, but also appear in cantatas, oratorios and even on their own (concert arias).
work_type_list.name: Aria

work_type_list.description: A ballet is music composed to be used, together with a choreography, for a ballet dance production.
work_type_list.name: Ballet

work_type_list.description: A cantata is a vocal (often choral) composition with an instrumental (usually orchestral) accompaniment, typically in several movements.
work_type_list.name: Cantata

work_type_list.description: A concerto is a musical work for soloist(s) accompanied by an orchestra.
work_type_list.name: Concerto

work_type_list.description: "Sonata" is a general term used to describe small scale (very often solo or solo + keyboard) instrumental works, initially in baroque music.
work_type_list.name: Sonata

work_type_list.description: A suite is an ordered set of instrumental or orchestral pieces normally performed in a concert setting. They may be extracts from a ballet or opera, or entirely original movements.
work_type_list.name: Suite

work_type_list.description: The madrigal is a type of secular vocal music composition. In its original form, it had no instrumental accompaniment, although accompaniment is much more common in later madrigals.
work_type_list.name: Madrigal

work_type_list.description: A mass is a choral composition that sets the invariable portions of the Christian Eucharistic liturgy (Kyrie - Gloria - Credo - Sanctus - Benedictus - Agnus Dei, with other portions sometimes added) to music.
work_type_list.name: Mass

work_type_list.description: "Motet" is a term that applies to different types of (usually unaccompanied) choral works. What exactly is a motet depends quite a bit on the period.
work_type_list.name: Motet

work_type_list.description: An opera is a dramatised work (text + musical score) for singers and orchestra/ensemble. In true operas all dialog is sung, through arias and recitatives, but some styles of opera include spoken dialogue.
work_type_list.name: Opera

work_type_list.description: An oratorio is a large (usually sacred) musical composition including an orchestra, a choir, and soloists. While it has characters and a plot, it is usually not performed theatrically (it lacks costumes, props and strong character interaction).
work_type_list.name: Oratorio

work_type_list.description: An overture is, generally, the instrumental introduction to an opera. Independent ("concert") overtures also exist, which are generally programmatic works shorter than a symphonic poem.
work_type_list.name: Overture

work_type_list.description: A partita is an instrumental piece composed of a series of variations, and it's by its current definition very similar to a suite.
work_type_list.name: Partita

work_type_list.description: A quartet is a musical composition scored for four voices or instruments.
work_type_list.name: Quartet

work_type_list.description: A song cycle is a group of songs designed to be performed in a sequence as a single entity. In most cases, all of the songs are by the same composer, and often use words from the same poet or lyricist.
work_type_list.name: Song-cycle

work_type_list.description: A symphony is an extended composition, almost always scored for orchestra without soloists.
work_type_list.name: Symphony

work_type_list.description: A song is in its origin (and still in most cases) a composition for voice, with or without instruments, performed by singing. This is the most common form by far in folk and popular music, but also fairly common in a classical context ("art songs").
work_type_list.name: Song

work_type_list.description: A symphonic poem is a piece of programmatic orchestral music, usually in a single movement, that evokes a painting, a landscape, the content of a poem, a story or novel, or other non-musical source.
work_type_list.name: Symphonic poem

work_type_list.description: A zarzuela is a Spanish lyric-dramatic work that alternates between spoken and sung scenes, the latter incorporating operatic and popular song, as well as dance.
work_type_list.name: Zarzuela

work_type_list.description: An étude is an instrumental musical composition, most commonly of considerable difficulty, usually designed to provide practice material for perfecting a particular technical skill.
work_type_list.name: Étude

work_type_list.description: A poem is a literary piece, generally short and in verse, where words are usually chosen for their sound and for the images and ideas they suggest.
work_type_list.name: Poem

work_type_list.description: A soundtrack is the music that accompanies a film, TV program, videogame, or even book.
work_type_list.name: Soundtrack

work_type_list.description: This represents literary works written in prose, that is, written in relatively ordinary language without metrical structure (e.g. novels, short stories, essays...).
work_type_list.name: Prose

work_type_list.description: The operetta is a genre of light opera, in terms both of music and subject matter. Operettas are generally short and include spoken parts.
work_type_list.name: Operetta

work_type_list.description: An audio drama is a dramatized, purely acoustic performance, broadcast on radio or published on an audio medium (tape, CD, etc.).
work_type_list.name: Audio drama

work_type_list.description: Beijing opera is a form of traditional Chinese theatre which combines music, vocal performance, mime, dance, and acrobatics.
work_type_list.name: Beijing opera

work_type_list.description: A play is a form of literature usually consisting of scripted dialogue between characters, and intended for theatrical performance rather than just reading.
work_type_list.name: Play

work_type_list.description: Musical theatre is a form of theatrical performance that combines songs, spoken dialogue, acting, and dance.
work_type_list.name: Musical

work_type_list.description: Incidental music is music written as background for (usually) a theatre play.
work_type_list.name: Incidental music
